perturbation.original_text: Drivers. The drivers for the 33 Winston Cup events to be held -------- during the 1998 season in which Prolong is an associate sponsor shall be Sterling Marlin (40), Joe Nemechek (42) and Wally Dallenbach Jr. (46), unless due to injury or for some unforeseeable reason, either is unable to drive, in which event Sabco shall choose a substitute driver to drive their respective Sabco/Prolong-sponsored entry. For the duration of this agreement, Sabco reserves the right to substitute or replace any of the aforementioned drivers listed without prior notice to Prolong or prior approval from Prolong.
perturbation.changed_text: Drivers. The drivers for the 33 Winston Cup events to be held -------- during the 1998 season in which Prolong is an associate sponsor shall be Sterling Marlin (40), Joe Nemechek (42) and Wally Dallenbach Jr. (46), unless due to injury or for some unforeseeable reason, either is unable to drive, in which event Sabco shall choose a substitute driver to drive their respective Sabco/Prolong-sponsored entry.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the final sentence in Section 2.A, Sabco now can not substitute/replace any of the aforementioned drivers without providing notice to Prolong. This affects the overall agreement, potentially devaluing Prolong's sponsorship if changes are made, due to the missing part.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Drivers. The drivers for the 33 Winston Cup events to be held -------- during the 1998 season in which Prolong is an associate sponsor shall be Sterling Marlin (40), Joe Nemechek (42) and Wally Dallenbach Jr. (46), unless due to injury or for some unforeseeable reason, either is unable to drive, in which event Sabco shall choose a substitute driver to drive their respective Sabco/Prolong-sponsored entry.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly granted Sabco the right to substitute drivers without prior notice or approval from Prolong. The changed text removes this right. The contradicted text, which is identical to the original text, implicitly assumes Sabco has this right. Therefore, the removal of Sabco's right to substitute drivers without notice in the changed text creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text because Sabco no longer has that explicit right.

perturbation.original_text: Sabco shall provide unto Prolong five aggregate ------------------ driver appearances (2) two-hours each at no fee. Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc. Additional appearances by Sabco drivers shall be at the discounted rate of [ * ] ($[ * ]) Dollars per day per driver, [ * ]. Sabco shall further provide the services of each driver for one free day for commercial use (up to six hours each) for still photo shoot, TV and radio/commercial production. Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc. All Sabco drivers, while under contract with Team SABCO, shall allow their likeness, voice, picture and signature to be used for commercial purposes endorsing Prolong at no additional costs during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Sabco shall provide unto Prolong five aggregate ------------------ driver appearances (2) two-hours each at no fee. Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc. Additional appearances by Sabco drivers shall be at the discounted rate of [ * ] ($[ * ]) Dollars per day per driver, [ * ]. Sabco shall further provide the services of each driver for one free day for commercial use (up to six hours each) for still photo shoot, TV and radio/commercial production. All Sabco drivers, while under contract with Team SABCO, shall allow their likeness, voice, picture and signature to be used for commercial purposes endorsing Prolong at no additional costs during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc." in section 3.0, creates a conflict, since Prolong now doesn't have to pay for travel expenses for the drivers, resulting in an in-text contradiction, if those driver appearances go over those allocated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sabco shall provide unto Prolong five aggregate ------------------ driver appearances (2) two-hours each at no fee. Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc. Additional appearances by Sabco drivers shall be at the discounted rate of [ * ] ($[ * ]) Dollars per day per driver, [ * ]. Sabco shall further provide the services of each driver for one free day for commercial use (up to six hours each) for still photo shoot, TV and radio/commercial production. All Sabco drivers, while under contract with Team SABCO, shall allow their likeness, voice, picture and signature to be used for commercial purposes endorsing Prolong at no additional costs during the term of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "Prolong shall be responsible for all first class travel expenses relating to each driver appearance, i.e. hotel, airfare, meals, etc." The changed text omits this sentence. This creates a contradiction, since the contradicted text assumes Prolong is responsible for these expenses. Now, there is no statement about who is responsible, directly contradicting the original allocation of responsibility, and creating a strong in-text contradiction due to the omission.

perturbation.original_text: In each of the 1998, 1999 and year-2000 races in which Prolong is designated as an associate sponsor, Sabco shall fax to Prolong officers on the Monday following the race, race results and a summary of the prior weekend activities.
perturbation.changed_text: In each of the 1998, 1999 and year-2000 races in which Prolong is designated as an associate sponsor, Sabco shall provide Prolong race results and a summary of the prior weekend activities.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "Sabco shall fax to Prolong officers on the Monday following the race", the modified text doesn't specify the method of delivery or the time frame, this create an in-text contradiction, since now the the race results and summary can be provided to Prolong at any given date/time, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Media, Public Relations and Sponsor Communications. Sabco will use its -------------------------------------------------- best efforts to obtain favorable exposure for Prolong and will be available to assist Prolong with public relations activities to a reasonable extent. This shall include making members of Sabco available for media interviews, press conferences or other public relations activity, as reasonably requested by Prolong, at or near race sites on dates Sabco is at race sites pursuant to their obligations hereunder. Sabco shall provide at its expense a staff member to handle race team's media and public relations needs and shall cause Prolong to receive prominent mention and display within press kits featuring the Sabco No. 40, 42 and 46 race cars. In each of the 1998, 1999 and year-2000 races in which Prolong is designated as an associate sponsor, Sabco shall provide Prolong race results and a summary of the prior weekend activities.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified 'Sabco shall fax to Prolong officers on the Monday following the race', creating a clear obligation for the method and timing of delivery. The changed text removes this specificity, stating only that 'Sabco shall provide Prolong race results'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the changed text no longer guarantees timely delivery or a specific delivery method, which may conflict with Prolong's operational needs or expectations as outlined in the Contradicted Text which is essentially the same as the changed text. The lack of a specified timeframe and method in the changed text contradicts any implicit or explicit assumptions in other parts of the agreement that rely on timely receipt of information via fax on the Monday following the race. Therefore, a strong contradiction is introduced.
